questions.question: Prove that equiangular triangles are similar.
questions.answer: Since all corresponding angles are equal, by AAA similarity criterion the triangles are similar.

questions.question: Explain why the converse of the Basic Proportionality Theorem is true.
questions.answer: If a line divides two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, it must be parallel to the third side, otherwise proportionality fails.

questions.question: Justify why AAA is sufficient for similarity but not for congruency.
questions.answer: AAA ensures same shape but not same size, hence similarity but not congruency.

questions.question: Explain why SAS similarity implies AAA similarity.
questions.answer: Once one angle and including sides are proportional, remaining angles become equal, satisfying AAA.

questions.question: Why does proportionality of sides lead to equality of angles?
questions.answer: Proportional sides create scaled triangles which preserve angular measures.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between scale factor and area ratio.
questions.answer: Area ratio equals the square of the scale factor.

questions.question: Why are similar triangles considered scaled versions of each other?
questions.answer: All corresponding sides are proportional and angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain the use of similarity in finding heights of objects.
questions.answer: Heights are calculated using proportional sides of similar triangles formed by shadows.

questions.question: Why is correspondence essential in similarity proofs?
questions.answer: Wrong correspondence leads to incorrect proportional relationships.

questions.question: Explain why similar figures preserve shape but not size.
questions.answer: All angles remain equal but sides change proportionally.

questions.question: Why are corresponding medians proportional in similar triangles?
questions.answer: Medians scale in the same ratio as the sides of similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain how triangle similarity is used in map reading.
questions.answer: Distances on maps are scaled versions of actual distances using similarity.

questions.question: Why is BPT fundamental to triangle similarity?
questions.answer: It establishes proportional sides which is essential for similarity.

questions.question: Explain why two triangles with equal areas need not be similar.
questions.answer: Equal areas do not guarantee proportional sides or equal angles.

questions.question: Discuss similarity as a geometric transformation.
questions.answer: Similarity enlarges or reduces figures while preserving shape.

questions.question: Why are all congruent triangles similar but not vice versa?
questions.answer: Congruent triangles satisfy all similarity conditions, but similar triangles may differ in size.

questions.question: Explain why two isosceles triangles are not always similar.
questions.answer: Their vertex angles or side ratios may differ.

questions.question: Why is angle-angle condition sufficient for triangle similarity?
questions.answer: The third angle automatically becomes equal due to angle sum property.

questions.question: Explain the role of similarity in surveying.
questions.answer: Surveying uses similar triangles to calculate inaccessible distances.

questions.question: Why is SAS similarity more restrictive than AA similarity?
questions.answer: It requires both angle equality and side proportionality.

questions.question: Explain why side ratios alone are not enough for polygon similarity.
questions.answer: Angles may differ even if side ratios are equal.

questions.question: Why is similarity important in engineering drawings?
questions.answer: It helps represent large objects accurately on smaller scales.

questions.question: Explain how similar triangles help prove Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: The theorem is derived using proportional sides of similar triangles.

questions.question: Why does similarity preserve angle measures?
questions.answer: Similarity transformations do not alter angular relationships.

questions.question: Explain why triangle similarity is central to geometry.
questions.answer: It forms the basis for proofs, constructions, and real-life applications.
